verdict_reasoning: SRC is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase canonically anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation, so baseline localization is cytoplasmic-face only and not accessible from outside. However, ecto-SRC / surface-displayed active SRC has been documented on tumor cells and in certain activated states, consistent with the broader cancer ecto-kinase phenomenon where nominally inner-leaflet kinases appear on the outer leaflet of transformed cells. This qualifies as cell-state-induced contextual surface display. Not classical surface receptor (no TM/GPI), not lysosomal exocytosis, not stable TM-partner tethering, not narrow germline lineage, not dual constitutive PM topology — the surface pool is tumor/activation-state restricted.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Strength and reproducibility of ecto-SRC surface display on tumor cells is limited; baseline biology is overwhelmingly inner-leaflet cytoplasmic.
